explanation: The requirement states that when Start-up Mode begins (i.e., 'StartUpMode'), the system 'shall go through an initialization process' that accomplishes (a) loading default parameters, (b) checking system memory, and (c) checking communication between controller and sensors/valves as well as between controller and GUI. The most relevant atomic propositions for these are: 'initStart' (initialization start), 'loadLog' (load parameters), 'checkCommsSensors' (check communication with sensors), 'checkCommsValves' (check communication with valves), and 'checkCommsGUI' (check communication with GUI). Since 'system memory check' does not have a direct atomic proposition, we focus on the other listed atomic actions. The most accurate way to express this in LTL, given the atomic proposition set, is to require that globally, if StartUpMode becomes true, then eventually, all the other required initialization actions happen. This can be captured as: G(StartUpMode -> (F(initStart) & F(loadLog) & F(checkCommsSensors) & F(checkCommsValves) & F(checkCommsGUI))). This formula ensures that, whenever Start-up Mode is initiated, eventually, all relevant initialization steps must occur. There are no better alternative LTL properties because sequencing or atomicity is not strictly specified in the English requirement nor can it be captured with the provided atomic propositions.

The natural language states that when Start-up Mode begins, the system must perform an initialization process that involves loading default parameters as well as checking system memory, communication with sensors and valves, and communication with the GUI. Given the available atomic propositions, we represent entering StartUpMode triggering (1) the controller initialization start, (2) loading parameters, (3) checking controller-sensor communication, (4) checking controller-valve communication, and (5) checking controller-GUI communication. These required checks and actions are best expressed by saying that after StartUpMode occurs, each of these initialization steps must eventually occur (regardless of their order). The translation thus uses the "implies" operator with G(StartUpMode -> (F initStart & F loadLog & F checkCommsSensors & F checkCommsValves & F checkCommsGUI)), stating that whenever StartUpMode is active, each process must eventually be observed.
output_LTL: G(StartUpMode -> (F initStart & F loadLog & F checkCommsSensors & F checkCommsValves & F checkCommsGUI))

explanation: The natural language says that when Start-up Mode begins (becomes true), the system must perform an initialization process that includes loading default parameters, checking system memory, checking communications between controller and sensors, valves, and GUI. The available APs that best fit are: StartUpMode (for start-up starting), loadLog (for loading parameters), checkCommsSensors (for checking communication with sensors), checkCommsValves (for checking communication with valves), checkCommsGUI (for checking communication with GUI), and initDone (to indicate the initialization is complete). System memory check is not explicitly mapped to an AP, but including all these APs ensures the logical property is as complete as possible given the mappings. The temporal property is: whenever StartUpMode occurs, eventually all the steps in the initialization process (loading parameters, communication checks) happen, and finally, initialization is done. This can be captured with: G (StartUpMode -> (F (loadLog & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI & initDone))). This captures the intent that these initialization events must eventually follow the start of StartUpMode, but does not demand they are all simultaneous, just that each must occur at some point after StartUpMode becomes true. Previous candidate formulae might have omitted some checks or not made clear that all actions must eventually follow on StartUpMode, but this explicitly includes all relevant APs in the property, tightly mapping to the specification.
output_LTL: G (StartUpMode -> F (loadLog & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI & initDone))

explanation: The requirement states that upon entering the 'Start-up Mode' (StartUpMode), the system must sequentially: (1) load default parameters, (2) check system memory, (3) check communication with sensors, (4) check communication with valves, and (5) check communication with the GUI. The atomic propositions closely related to these actions are: 'StartUpMode' (to detect mode entering), and (for checking and loading) 'loadLog' (system load parameters), 'checkCommsSensors', 'checkCommsValves', 'checkCommsGUI'. There is no atomic proposition for "check system memory", so it cannot be captured directly in the LTL with the given APs. The requirement is best formalized as: whenever StartUpMode is entered, eventually each of the steps (loadLog, checkCommsSensors, checkCommsValves, checkCommsGUI) will occur. Conjunction (&) of 'eventually' (F) is used to ensure all steps eventually occur. Thus, the LTL property is: G (StartUpMode -> (F loadLog & F checkCommsSensors & F checkCommsValves & F checkCommsGUI)). Previous properties might miss the 'eventually' semantics or fail to require all actions after StartUpMode; this property captures the essential sequential guarantee that all checks and loads must happen after StartUpMode starts.
output_LTL: G (StartUpMode -> (F loadLog & F checkCommsSensors & F checkCommsValves & F checkCommsGUI))

explanation: The LTL property must model that whenever StartUpMode is initiated, the system eventually performs an ordered set of actions: initialization starts, default parameters load, communication checks among hardware and GUI, and completes with the end of initialization. Each step should eventually follow the previous, but LTL does not support sequencing arbitrary sets, so we nest 'eventually' (F) to enforce an order. The correct formula avoids any extra parentheses and error-causing structures. This ensures that after StartUpMode is entered, the sequence of initStart, loadLog, checkCommsSensors, checkCommsValves, checkCommsGUI, and initDone all eventually occur, in that order. Previous LTLs had mismatched parentheses; the corrected formula below is syntactically valid LTL.
output_LTL: G (StartUpMode -> F (initStart & F (loadLog & F (checkCommsSensors & F (checkCommsValves & F (checkCommsGUI & F initDone))))))

explanation: The requirement describes that, whenever Start-up Mode is initiated (represented by the atomic proposition StartUpMode), the system is expected to perform several steps: go through an initialization process (initStart), load default parameters (loadLog), check the system memory (no direct proposition, but assumed to be part of self-test or initialization), check the communication between the controller and the sensors/valves (checkCommsSensors, checkCommsValves), and also between the controller and the GUI (checkCommsGUI). These actions are expected to be performed as a consequence of entering Start-up Mode, and prior to the system being ready (which could be indicated by initDone, but this is not in the original sentence). The LTL property is constructed to say: globally, whenever StartUpMode holds, then eventually each required check/init operation will occur. This is expressed as: G(StartUpMode -> (F initStart & F loadLog & F checkCommsSensors & F checkCommsValves & F checkCommsGUI)). This captures that on every occurrence of StartUpMode, all the necessary init/check actions will follow at some future point (including possibly immediately). If earlier LTL properties omitted any of these checks, or only required one or some of the steps, they would be incomplete. Here, all main parts of the described process are included.
output_LTL: G(StartUpMode -> (F initStart & F loadLog & F checkCommsSensors & F checkCommsValves & F checkCommsGUI))

explanation: The natural language statement specifies that when the 'Start-up Mode' is initiated (i.e., upon StartUpMode), the system must undergo initialization, which includes loading default parameters and checking system memory and communication lines (controller with sensors and valves, and with GUI). Among available atomic propositions, only those directly associated with the described process are relevant: StartUpMode (for mode initiation), initStart/initDone/initFail (for start and end/failure of initialization), loadLog (for loading parameters), checkCommsSensors, checkCommsValves, and checkCommsGUI (for communications checks). To represent that, once StartUpMode occurs, the initialization process (initStart), loading parameters (loadLog), and the relevant checks must all eventually happen before initialization concludes (initDone) or fails (initFail), we require that these occur between the mode starting and the process finishing (or failing). This can be captured best with an implication from StartUpMode to the eventual occurrence (F, or 'eventually') of all necessary actions before conclusion (initDone|initFail). Previous attempts at encoding may have directly used sequencing ('until') but would miss the necessity of all steps; thus, the conjunction inside 'F' ensures all needed actions take place. The chosen LTL formula below mandates that whenever StartUpMode is activated, at some point afterwards and before initialization concludes (either success or failure), initialization starts, parameters are loaded, and all relevant communications checks (sensors, valves, GUI) are performed.
output_LTL: G( StartUpMode -> (F( (initStart & loadLog & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI) & (initDone | initFail) ) ) )

explanation: The input states that upon the Start-up Mode being initiated, the system must execute an initialization process, which includes loading default parameters and checking system memory, as well as communications with sensors, valves, and the GUI. Among the available atomic propositions, 'StartUpMode' indicates entry to start-up, and 'initStart' suggests initialization begins, while 'initDone' signals the completion of configuration loading and internal checking. The checks for communication correspond to 'checkCommsSensors,' 'checkCommsValves,' and 'checkCommsGUI,' and 'loadLog' can represent loading parameters. To ensure that, after StartUpMode is entered, these actions (initStart, loadLog, checkCommsSensors, checkCommsValves, checkCommsGUI) happen before the initialization completes (initDone), the LTL expresses that once StartUpMode holds, all those checks (and loading) must happen before initDone can be true. Thus, 'StartUpMode -> ((!(initDone)) U (initStart & loadLog & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI & initDone))' is used: when StartUpMode begins, the listed steps must occur before initialization is done. Previous LTLs might omit some required checks or the temporal order that the checks and loading must all complete before 'initDone' occurs, but this version explicitly links StartUpMode to a required sequence involving all relevant atomic propositions.
output_LTL: G (StartUpMode -> ((!(initDone)) U (initStart & loadLog & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI & initDone)))

explanation: The natural language requires that whenever Start-up Mode is initiated (i.e., when StartUpMode becomes true), the system must go through an initialization sequence that 1) loads default parameters, and checks: (a) system memory, (b) controller's communication with the sensors, (c) controller's communication with the valves, and (d) controller's communication with the GUI. Given the available atomic propositions, 'initStart' denotes system initialization start, and 'initDone' denotes successful completion. The individual checks correspond to 'checkCommsSensors', 'checkCommsValves', and 'checkCommsGUI', and loading parameters is captured by 'loadLog'. Thus, after StartUpMode is initiated, it must be the case that each of these steps (initStart, loadLog, checkCommsSensors, checkCommsValves, checkCommsGUI) eventually occur before initialization completes ('initDone'). This is most precisely captured by stating: after StartUpMode becomes true, all subprocesses are required to happen (eventually) before initDone. The LTL below formalizes that if StartUpMode occurs, the sequence from initStart through the various checks and load must take place (order of some steps is not strictly specified, so 'eventually' covers them), and 'initDone' cannot occur before all have happened.
output_LTL: G (StartUpMode -> (F initStart & F loadLog & F checkCommsSensors & F checkCommsValves & F checkCommsGUI & ( (initStart & loadLog & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI) U initDone ) ) )

explanation: The given natural language requirement describes a sequence of events that must occur upon the initiation of 'Start-up Mode': (1) system enters StartUpMode, (2) the system begins initialization (initStart), (3) the default parameters are loaded (loadLog), (4) communication with sensors and valves is checked (checkCommsSensors, checkCommsValves), (5) communication between GUI and controller is checked (checkCommsGUI), and (6) initialization is successfully completed (initDone). This sequence must happen each time StartUpMode is initiated. Thus, in LTL, globally whenever StartUpMode starts, it is followed (in order) by the initialization process, loading of parameters, checking communication (with sensors, valves, and GUI), and successful completion. The LTL below uses the release (V) and until (U) operators to ensure ordering. Previous translations may have failed to capture the complete order of operations or may not have required all the communication checks prior to 'initDone', but in this translation, the entire sequence and all communication checks are enforced.
output_LTL: G( StartUpMode -> ( initStart U ( loadLog & ( checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI ) U initDone ) ) )
